title: Agent Definition Schema
description: Schema for defining a new agent in the AI development studio

name.description: Lowercase-kebab-case identifier for the agent. Used in API calls, branch prefixes, and file paths. Examples: 'backend-dev', 'frontend-dev', 'qa', 'pm', 'evo'

display_name.description: Human-readable name for the agent. Examples: 'Backend Developer', 'Frontend Developer', 'QA Agent'

type.description: Agent role category. Helps identify agent responsibilities and relationships.

mission.description: One-line mission statement describing the agent's primary purpose. Used as heading in CLAUDE.md

scope.description: What this agent is responsible for. Each item is a domain or responsibility area.

not_scope.description: Explicitly what this agent does NOT own. Prevents scope creep and clarifies boundaries.

tech_stack.description: Primary technologies and languages this agent works with. Optional; may vary per project.

tools.description: Additional tools, CLIs, or utilities this agent can use beyond standard development tools.

skills.description: Paths to shared skill files in the knowledge base. These are reusable workflows or knowledge artifacts.

constitution_focus.description: Which principles from /constitution.md are most critical for this agent's role. Used to emphasize key values in CLAUDE.md.

api_permissions.description: Which API endpoints this agent is allowed to call. Defines their interaction capabilities.

branch_prefix.description: Git branch prefix pattern for this agent's work. Used by PM to create and manage branches.

reporting_to.description: Which agent(s) this agent reports status to. Typically PM for individual contributors; humans for leadership roles.

collaborates_with.description: Which other agents this agent frequently collaborates with.

communication_norms.description: How this agent communicates with others.

response_time_hours.description: Expected time to respond to questions/issues

escalation_path.description: How issues escalate when agent can't resolve

success_metrics.description: How to measure if this agent is performing well. Used by EVO for analysis.
examples: PR review cycle time (days from open to merged)
Defect rate (issues found by QA per 100 LOC)

constraints.description: Constraints or limitations on this agent's work.
examples: Cannot make hiring or team decisions

claude_md_path.description: Path to this agent's CLAUDE.md file (the system prompt/constitution for a Claude Code session running as this agent)

examples.description: Real examples of what a session with this agent would do.
examples: Reads task, checks KB, writes backend code, runs tests, opens PR
Develops test plan, executes tests, reports issues, tracks metrics

mvp_agents.description: The 5 MVP agents in the studio

examples.name: pm
examples.mission: Own PRD lifecycle, decompose tasks, coordinate dependencies, track progress

examples.name: backend-dev
examples.mission: Build APIs, database logic, integrations with comprehensive testing

examples.name: frontend-dev
examples.mission: Build accessible, performant UI and pages

examples.name: qa
examples.mission: Develop test plans, execute tests, identify issues, track quality metrics

examples.name: evo
examples.mission: Analyze processes, identify bottlenecks, recommend improvements, measure impact

example.name: backend-dev
example.mission: Build and maintain server-side logic, APIs, database queries, and integrations with best practices, comprehensive testing, and clear documentation.

communication_norms.primary_channel: REST API messages and PR comments

success_metrics: PR cycle time (average days from open to merged)
Defect rate from QA (issues per 100 LOC)
constraints: Cannot deploy to production (PM coordinates deployment)
Cannot merge own PRs (PM owns merge decision)
Must write tests for all code (non-negotiable)
examples: 1. Gets task from PM via API; reads acceptance criteria and dependencies
2. Checks KB for relevant patterns (@kb: api-design-standards, @kb: testing-strategy)
4. Writes API endpoint with tests (target >80% coverage)
5. Opens PR with clear description, database migrations, performance notes
6. Responds to code review feedback; fixes issues in new commits
7. Coordinates with QA; responds to test failures; marks task done after merge
